item.title: Container user '1000:1000' hardcoded in 10+ locations across 6 files
item.intent: Container user '1000:1000' has a single source of truth in config/defaults.ts. No literal '1000:1000' or '1000' string appears in runtime code outside that file. Changing the container user UID/GID requires editing one line.
item.description: The container user string '1000:1000' is hardcoded in at least 10 locations across 6 files: src/build/docker/dockerfile.ts (lines 77, 121, 135, 142 — chown and USER directives), src/build/docker/posture.ts (lines 21, 31, 41, 51 — all four security posture configs), src/design/configure/generate.ts:34 (local const CONTAINER_USER = '1000:1000' — defines it but doesn't export), src/operate/doctor/checks.ts:373,378 (container user validation), src/operate/doctor/fix.ts:180,187,189 (container user auto-fix), src/config/validate.ts:146,153,154 (LM-06 landmine validation). Same BUG-011/BUG-025 constant-scattering pattern. generate.ts already defines CONTAINER_USER locally — it just doesn't export or share it. Steps: (1) add CONTAINER_USER = '1000:1000' to src/config/defaults.ts with comment 'Container non-root user (LM-06 landmine requirement)'; (2) replace the local const in generate.ts with an import from defaults.ts; (3) import CONTAINER_USER in dockerfile.ts, posture.ts, doctor/checks.ts, doctor/fix.ts, config/validate.ts and replace all hardcoded '1000:1000' strings; (4) run full test suite. Scope: 7 files, no logic change. Pure constant extraction.
